summary: The test suite covers all seven public API functions end-to-end and maps cleanly to every stated requirement: hashing, indexing, diffing, plan generation, dry-run, execute, and full integration. Section 4 (New-SyncPlan) is especially well-isolated, using inline PSCustomObject mocks rather than real file trees. BeforeAll/AfterAll scoping is correct and cleanup is reliable. The main weaknesses are in rigor: several important edge cases are absent — empty source or dest directories, dry-run's ActionsPerformed not being asserted to equal 0, no verification that dry-run Actions all carry Status='Planned', no test for error recovery during execute mode (e.g., unreadable source file), no handling of case-sensitivity differences, and no coverage of symlinks or special characters in paths. Section 7 also has a subtle test-ordering dependency: the 'execute mode' It block assumes the dry-run block ran first and left the dest in its pre-sync state, making the two tests stateful siblings rather than independent — if order ever changes the second test would fail spuriously. Fixing the dry-run ActionsPerformed assertion and decoupling the E2E tests into their own BeforeEach setup would raise both rigor and design noticeably.
